title: [CI:DOCS] VOLUME needs to be declared after CHOWN
body:
are set. Not doing do will cause:
https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=2009266

Signed-off-by: Jindrich Novy <jnovy@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name.  Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

comments.body: Build tests are red, unclear exactly why

I disagree with this, this change will cause these directories to be owned by root, I believe.  Podman looks at the ownership of the destination directory and makes the source match the destination.  We want to make sure that /home/podman/... content is all owned by the podman user.  I believe you change will make the newly created volume be owned by root.

@rhatdan it is actually the right opposite - /home/podman/.local/share/containers is owned by root:root because it is created right after VOLUME in the Dockerfile. This why https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=2009266 is reported.

With original Dockerfile:
```
$ podman run -it --rm test:test ls -ld /home/podman/.local/share/containers
drwxr-xr-x. 2 root root 6 Oct 15 10:16 /home/podman/.local/share/containers
```

With this PR applied to the Dockerfile:
```
$ podman run -it --rm test:test ls -ld /home/podman/.local/share/containers
drwxr-xr-x. 2 podman podman 6 Oct 15 10:14 /home/podman/.local/share/containers
```

If I use the actual Podman we ship, I see
```
$ podman run quay.io/podman/stable ls -ld /home/podman/.local/share/containers /var/lib/containers
drwxr-xr-x. 1 podman podman  0 Oct 14 08:07 /home/podman/.local/share/containers
drwxr-xr-x. 1 root   root   16 Oct 14 08:07 /var/lib/containers
```
Which is what I would expect.

What you are writing and what the PR do are different.

You PR will do

RUN chown -R podman:podman /home/podman
VOLUME /home/podman/.local/share/containers

Where currently we do.
VOLUME /home/podman/.local/share/containers
RUN chown -R podman:podman /home/podman

There seems to be a regression somewhere in podman. When I build the non-RHEL image directly from upstream using podman-3.4.0-1.fc34.x86_64 I get this:

```
$ podman run -it --rm test:test ls -ld /home/podman/.local/share/containers
drwxr-xr-x. 2 root root 6 Oct 18 06:06 /home/podman/.local/share/containers
```

I am doing the tests with

 podman -v
podman version 3.4.0
Could you make sure you have the latest image?

Also mount up the image you have and check the underlying directory.

podman unshare
mnt=podman image mount quay.io/podman/stable
ls -ld $mnt/home/podman/.local/share/containers

podman unshare
mnt=$(podman image mount quay.io/podman/stable)
ls -ld $mnt/home/podman/.local/share/containers
drwxr-xr-x. 2 jnovy jnovy 6 Oct 17 10:05 /home/jnovy/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/de38dbe0d42b232b16a8e290d6860d657d4010f237f727d29884e74f52f2f15b/merged/home/podman/.local/share/containers
Could you attach the Dockerfile you are using?

The original Dockerfile showing this bug in RHEL8.5:
[Dockerfile.txt](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/7487033/Dockerfile.txt)

The fixed Dockerfile for RHEL8.5:
[Dockerfile-fixed.txt](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/7487067/Dockerfile-fixed.txt)

@jnovy: PR needs rebase.
